top_categories.workout_context: 1118 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
88,583ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 1118 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At Race Through Poland, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88,583ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Race Through Poland's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
1118 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 88,583ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Low-cadence force work for 88,583ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Race Through Poland. Train it until it feels boring.
Race Through Poland's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 1118 miles.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 1118 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 1118 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Race Through Poland with 88,583ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and Endurance to prepare you for 1118 miles of mixed terrain in Nowy Targ, Lesser Poland.
